CheckTitle: Conditional Access policies enforce MFA for device registration
Description:
Microsoft Entra **Conditional Access** policies can require **multifactor authentication (MFA)** for **device registration** operations.

This control ensures users must complete MFA before **registering or joining devices** to the directory, reducing the likelihood that compromised credentials can be used to register rogue devices.
Risk: Without MFA for device registration, attackers with stolen credentials could register unauthorized devices into the directory, gain persistence, and bypass compliance-based Conditional Access protections that rely on trusted device state.

Code.Other:
1. Navigate to the Microsoft Entra admin center (https://entra.microsoft.com).
2. Expand **Protection** > **Conditional Access** and select **Policies**.
3. Click **New policy**.
4. Under **Users**, include **All users**.
5. Under **Target resources**, select **User actions** and check **Register or join devices**.
6. Under **Grant**, select **Grant access** and require **multifactor authentication**.
7. Set the policy to **Report-only** until validated, then enable it.

Recommendation.Text: Enforce **MFA** through **Conditional Access** for **device registration** so users must verify identity before registering or joining devices to the directory.

Notes: Requires Entra ID P1 or later license.
